skill.name: prd-to-appspec
skill.description: Transform Product Requirements Documents into structured XML app specifications for AI coding agents. The skill extracts requirements, constraints, data models, features, implementation steps, and success criteria from a PRD.
skill.summary: Convert technical PRDs into agent-ready XML application specifications.
skill.author: AaronAbuUsama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found they are Markdown examples, XML templates, inline code ticks, and prose references. No executable scripts, malicious network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration intent were found. The main residual risk is expected local filesystem access to read a user-provided PRD and write an app_spec.txt output.

low_findings.title: Expected Local File Read And Output Write
low_findings.description: The workflow asks for a PRD file path, reads that document, and writes app_spec.txt to a default or user-selected output path. This is expected for the skill purpose, but users should review paths before granting filesystem access. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for filesystem access, low risk. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: The lines explicitly describe reading a PRD path and writing the output file, but there is no evidence of stealthy access or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly reads a user-provided PRD path and writes an output file. The context is legitimate document conversion, with no evidence of stealth or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Network Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command, weak-crypto, and network indicators occur inside instructional Markdown, XML examples, inline code ticks, or illustrative Python snippets. They are not executable skill code and do not instruct the agent to run shell commands or contact external services. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for external command, weak cryptography, and network execution findings. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The flagged locations are documentation examples such as output templates, transformation rules, and sample fetch/error-handling text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the static matches are literal Markdown examples and inline formatting. No executable source files or command execution instructions were found.

content.user_title: Convert PRDs Into Agent-Ready App Specs
content.value_statement: Technical PRDs often mix requirements with implementation details that are hard for coding agents to use directly. This skill converts them into structured XML specifications with clear features, constraints, steps, and success criteria.
actual_capabilities: Identifies PRD sections such as overview, technology stack, features, data models, APIs, tests, and implementation plans.
Converts implementation-heavy content into declarative requirements and constraints.
Transforms model definitions into database or data schema descriptions.
Summarizes CLI, API, and library interfaces without preserving raw implementation code.
Builds numbered implementation steps and measurable success criteria.
Produces an XML-style app_spec.txt structure for downstream coding agents.
limitations: The skill depends on the quality and completeness of the source PRD.
Ambiguous requirements may still need user clarification before final output.
It does not validate generated XML with a parser by itself.
It may omit domain details that are only implied and not written in the PRD.

use_cases.title: Prepare A Build Spec From A PRD
use_cases.description: Convert a long technical PRD into an app_spec.txt that a coding agent can follow during implementation.

use_cases.title: Normalize Requirements For Agent Handoffs
use_cases.description: Turn mixed code examples, user stories, and test plans into a consistent XML requirements document.

use_cases.title: Extract Acceptance Criteria From Tests
use_cases.description: Translate test assertions and expected outputs into measurable success criteria for delivery review.

prompt_templates.title: Convert A Simple PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a known file path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert PRD.md into an app_spec.txt. Preserve requirements, constraints, implementation steps, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Use A Custom Output Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants control over the destination path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert docs/product-prd.md into an app spec and write the result to prompts/app_spec.txt.

prompt_templates.title: Focus On Agent Usability
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a coding-agent handoff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Transform this PRD into an app specification for Claude Code and Codex. Remove code snippets and preserve all constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Completeness During Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PRD into an XML app spec, then report missing requirements, ambiguous decisions, and sections that need clarification.

output_examples.input: A PRD with Pydantic models, feature descriptions, API notes, and test assertions.
output_examples.output: An app specification with project overview, technology stack, data schema descriptions, feature groups, implementation milestones, and success criteria.

output_examples.input: A PRD that includes function bodies, error handling examples, and CLI usage samples.
output_examples.output: A declarative specification that describes behavior, constraints, commands, expected errors, and measurable outcomes without raw code.

output_examples.input: A partial PRD with unclear technology choices and missing acceptance criteria.
output_examples.output: A draft app spec plus a transformation summary that highlights gaps requiring user clarification.

best_practices: Provide the full PRD path and desired output path before starting conversion.
Review the generated app spec for missing constraints, vague features, and unsupported assumptions.
Keep implementation code out of the final specification unless it is needed as a named interface.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to execute code from a PRD.
Do not treat generated implementation steps as final engineering estimates.
Do not remove user-facing requirements just because the PRD also contains code examples.

faq.question: What input does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs a PRD file or PRD text with requirements, features, technology choices, models, APIs, tests, or implementation notes.

faq.question: What does the skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces an XML-style application specification intended for AI coding agents and implementation planning.

faq.question: Does it execute code from the PRD?
faq.answer: No. The workflow describes conversion of PRD content into requirements. It does not require executing source code.

faq.question: Can it handle incomplete PRDs?
faq.answer: Yes, but the output may include gaps or clarification needs when required sections are missing.

faq.question: Is the output limited to web applications?
faq.answer: No. The workflow supports product-focused apps, system-focused projects, libraries, CLI tools, and API services.

faq.question: Why use XML-style output?
faq.answer: Structured tags make sections easier for coding agents to parse, compare, and follow during implementation.
